revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:彦湯支命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|father={{ill|Umashimaji|qid=Q10946169|1=Umashimaji}}}}
{{Nobility infobox | name = Hikoyuki no Mikoto (彦湯支命) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Hikoyuji no Mikoto, Hikoyuki no Mikoto, Kike no Sukune | titles = [[Sukune|Ashina]] (足尼), Lord of State Affairs (申食国大夫) | allegiance = [[Emperor Suizei]] | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|qid=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji no Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウマシマジ}}<ref name="tenson">"Tenson Honki," {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}.</ref>; Mother: Shinaga-hime<ref name="tenson"/> | siblings = Brother: {{ill|Amanigiyu no Mikoto|ja|味饒田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanigiyu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="tenson"/> | spouse = Ano-hime<ref name="tenson"/>, Izumo Irotari-hime<ref name="tenson"/>, Omi Kagawa-karehime<ref name="tenson"/> | children = {{ill|Ooneno Mikoto|ja|大禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooneno Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="tenson"/>, {{ill|Izumo Iro Daijin no Mikoto|ja|出雲色大臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Iro Daijin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="tenson"/>, {{ill|Izushi Kokoro Daijin no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izushi Kokoro Daijin no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref name="tenson"/> }}
'''Hikoyuki no Mikoto''' (彦湯支命, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese noble associated with the {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|qid=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}}. He is also referred to by the alternate name "Kike no Sukune" (木開足尼).<ref name="tenson"/>
== Overview ==
According to the "Tenson Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, Hikoyuki no Mikoto first became {{ill|Sukune|lt=Sukune|lt=Ashina|ja|足尼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashina|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宿禰}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suizei|en|Emperor Suizei|qid=Q313321|lt=Emperor Suizei|1=Emperor Suizei}}, later ascending to the role of Lord of State Affairs (申食国政大夫) while enshrining the deity of {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|qid=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine|1=Isonokami Jingū}}. The "Tenson Honki" also records that in the third year of Suizei's reign, Hikoyuki no Mikoto, son of Umashimaji no Mikoto, was appointed as Lord of State Affairs.<ref name="tenson"/>
The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} refers to him as "Hikoyuji no Mikoto" and "Hikoyuki no Mikoto," identifying him as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki clan|ja|志貴連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} and the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}} in {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}.
His name, Hikoyuki (湯支), is phonetically linked to "Yuki" (悠紀), a key concept in {{ill|Daijosai|en|Daijosai|qid=Q11433653|lt=Daijosai|1=Daijosai}} rituals, suggesting a ceremonial role in rice cultivation and harvests. Scholars argue that his brother's name, Amanigiyu no Mikoto, meaning "fertile fields," and Hikoyuki's responsibilities reflect the agricultural functions traditionally associated with the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|qid=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}}.<ref name="kuroda">{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda |date=2014-12 |url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548 |title=A Restorative Study on the Mononobe Clan Lineage in "Tenson Honki" (I) |journal=Osaka Prefecture University Technical College Research Bulletin |ISSN=2186-2826 |publisher=Osaka Prefecture University |volume=48 |page=5 |doi=10.24729/00007548 |hdl=10466/14200}}</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Hikoyuki no Mikoto was the son of {{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji no Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimaji no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウマシマジ}} and Shinaga-hime, daughter of Ikume Mura’s Iyo Kuremomo. His elder brother was Amanigiyu no Mikoto. Hikoyuki married Ano-hime, with whom he fathered {{ill|Ooneno Mikoto|ja|大禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooneno Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and Omi Kagawa-karehime, who bore him {{ill|Izushi Kokoro Daijin no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izushi Kokoro Daijin no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto|qq=}}.<ref name="tenson"/>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11665973}}
{{translated page|ja|彦湯支命|version=103125644|comment=Imported full ja history}}
